question: Will the highest temperature in Seattle be 45°F or below on March 22?
description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Seattle-Tacoma International Airport Station in degrees Fahrenheit on 22 Mar '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Seattle-Tacoma International Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/us/wa/seatac/KSEA.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Fahrenheit (eg, 21°F). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment for Seattle's highest temperature on March 22 clusters tightly around 52-53°F (46%) and 50-51°F (42%), driven by NOAA's latest forecast models projecting a daytime high of 51-53°F under partly cloudy skies with light onshore flow. Ensemble guidance from GFS and ECMWF shows minimal spread, with a weak upper-level ridge allowing partial afternoon clearing after morning marine stratus, potentially boosting peaks by 1-2°F via increased insolation. Differentiating the bins: persistent low clouds favor 50-51°F, while earlier burn-off aligns with 52-53°F, matching March climatology (average high 54°F) amid neutral ENSO conditions; traders await 18Z model updates for resolution clarity.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus overwhelmingly favors a Chicago high of 68-69°F on March 22, driven by National Weather Service forecasts pinpointing a sunny day with south-southwest winds of 5-10 mph yielding a precise peak near 68°F, corroborated by ensemble models from GFS and ECMWF showing tight clustering around this value. This positioning aligns with recent upper-air analyses indicating a warming ridge over the Midwest, elevating temps well above March's typical 45°F average but within observed variability for late-winter warmouts. Realistic challenges include a stronger southerly surge pushing into 70-71°F territory if winds exceed 15 mph, or minor model cool biases resolving warmer; downside to 66°F remains possible via unexpected marine layer, though current guidance deems higher outliers improbable.

question: Counter-Strike: MOUZ NXT vs Alpha Dominion Nation (BO1) - DraculaN Group A
description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike match between MOUZ NXT and Alpha Dominion Nation in the DraculaN Group A, initially scheduled for March 29 at 5:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "MOUZ NXT" if MOUZ NXT win the match against Alpha Dominion Nation.

This market will resolve to "Alpha Dominion Nation" if Alpha Dominion Nation win the match against MOUZ NXT.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

eventMetadata.context_description: MOUZ NXT holds a decisive edge as the top seed in DraculaN Group A, driving trader consensus toward an 80% implied win probability in this high-variance BO1 against underdog Alpha Dominion Nation. The academy squad's recent dominance—3-0 sweeps in qualifiers and a flawless group stage run—stems from siuhy and Jimpphat's rifling prowess on maps like Mirage and Inferno, where they've posted 1.25+ HLTV ratings. Alpha Dominion struggles with inconsistency, dropping key clutches in losses to lower-tier foes, exacerbated by lineup flux and no head-to-head edge. Watch for map vetoes and momentum swings, as BO1 upsets spike 25% in tier-2 events, but NXT's depth and home-like server advantage solidify their favoritism.

question: Map 1: Odd/Even Total Kills?
description:
This market will resolve to "Odd" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 1 is an odd number.

This market will resolve to "Even" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 1 is an even number.

"Total combined kills" includes all kills recorded during the match across all rounds, including kills during regulation rounds and overtime rounds if applicable. Team kills (friendly fire) and self-inflicted deaths (e.g., falling damage, own grenade) do not count as kills for the purposes of this market.

If no kills are recorded in Map 1, or if Map 1 is canceled (not played at all), or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Map 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

This market will resolve to "Odd" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 1 is an odd number.

This market will resolve to "Even" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 1 is an even number.

"Total combined kills" includes all kills recorded during the match across all rounds, including kills during regulation rounds and overtime rounds if applicable. Team kills (friendly fire) and self-inflicted deaths (e.g., falling damage, own grenade) do not count as kills for the purposes of this market.

If no kills are recorded in Map 1, or if Map 1 is canceled (not played at all), or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Map 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Counter-Strike: Eternal Fire vs Drama eSports (BO1) - DraculaN Group D
events.description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike match between Eternal Fire and Drama eSports in the DraculaN Group D, initially scheduled for March 29 at 6:30AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Eternal Fire" if Eternal Fire win the match against Drama eSports.

This market will resolve to "Drama eSports" if Drama eSports win the match against Eternal Fire.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: Eternal Fire enters as heavy favorites in this BO1 Group D clash at DraculaN, with trader consensus implying over 80% win probability driven by their superior firepower led by AWPer XANTARES, who's boasting a 1.25 rating over the last five maps. Recent form favors the Turks, who whitewashed rivals 2-0 in their prior qualifier while Drama eSports struggles with a 1-4 record in Tier 2 events, hampered by inconsistent rifling from their IGL. No roster changes reported, but Eternal Fire's map pool depth—strong on Mirage and Inferno—gives them edge in single-map volatility; watch for Drama's upset potential via aggressive Ancient picks amid Eternal's occasional CT-side leaks. Momentum and head-to-head absence tilt odds firmly.

question: Map 1: Odd/Even Total Rounds?
description:
This market will resolve to "Odd" if the total combined rounds played by both teams in Map 1 is an odd number.

This market will resolve to "Even" if the total combined rounds played by both teams in Map 1 is an even number.

question: Will USD reach 1.6M Iranian rials by March 31?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the finalized USD exchange rate on Bonbast is equal to or above the specified price for any day between market creation and March 31, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve according to the daily finalized free-market USD exchange rate as displayed on Bonbast (https://www.bonbast.com/graph/usd), which publishes prices in Iranian toman, where 1 Iranian toman equals 10 Iranian rials (IRR).

A daily figure will be considered finalized once the following day’s figure is released.

Revisions or corrections to daily figures indicating a qualifying exchange rate will be considered only if they occur before all relevant figures for this market have been finalized.

The resolution source for this market will be Bonbast (https://www.bonbast.com/graph/usd). Resolution will occur once the March 31, 2026, exchange rate data is finalized. If the resolution source becomes permanently unavailable, another resolution source will be chosen.

question: Counter-Strike: Lazer Cats vs aimclub (BO1) - DraculaN Group A
description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike match between Lazer Cats and aimclub in the DraculaN Group A, initially scheduled for March 29 at 5:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Lazer Cats" if Lazer Cats win the match against aimclub.

This market will resolve to "aimclub" if aimclub win the match against Lazer Cats.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

question: Map 1: Odd/Even Total Kills?
description:
This market will resolve to "Odd" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 1 is an odd number.

This market will resolve to "Even" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 1 is an even number.

"Total combined kills" includes all kills recorded during the match across all rounds, including kills during regulation rounds and overtime rounds if applicable. Team kills (friendly fire) and self-inflicted deaths (e.g., falling damage, own grenade) do not count as kills for the purposes of this market.

If no kills are recorded in Map 1, or if Map 1 is canceled (not played at all), or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Map 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

question: New "Stranger Things" episode released by March 31?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Netflix officially releases a new episode of Stranger Things (i.e., an episode that was not previously available to stream on Netflix) between market creation and March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market, an “episode” must be listed as a distinct episode of Stranger Things on Netflix and be playable for general subscribers in the United States. A behind-the-scenes featurette, documentary, trailer, recap, cast interview, deleted scenes compilation, or other bonus content will not count unless it is clearly presented by Netflix as an official numbered or titled episode of the series.

If Netflix releases an alternate cut, extended cut, or “secret” version of an already-released episode, it will not count unless it is listed as a separate episode entry on Netflix.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the Stranger Things title page on Netflix (episode list and availability), however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

question: ChatGPT Outage by March 27?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if OpenAI's ChatGPT experiences any incident classified as 'Partial/Full Outage' as of the time it is marked as “Resolved” by the listed date (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Only incidents listing ChatGPT under 'Affected components' will be considered. Incidents labeled as affecting 'APIs,' or 'Sora,' but not ChatGPT, will have no bearing on the resolution of this market.

Classifications of an incident while it is ongoing will have no bearing on the resolution of this market. Only classifications of events that are resolved will be considered.

Qualifying incidents include outages and other issues classified as 'Partial/Full Outage' when they are resolved, during this market's above-specified timeframe. 

An incident resolved outside this market’s timeframe will only qualify if ongoing at this market’s resolution time, in which case the market will remain open until that incident is marked as “Resolved,” and resolution will be based on the first impact classification thereafter, regardless of subsequent revisions or corrections.

Revisions that upgrade an incident’s impact classification to 'Partial/Full Outage' will qualify if the incident was resolved and the revision is published within this market’s timeframe.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official system status information published by OpenAI on status.openai.com; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

question: Critical Discord Incident by April 30, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Discord experiences any incident classified as Critical (red) by the listed date, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Qualifying incidents may include outages and other issues that Discord classifies as Critical.

Revisions to the impact classification of any such incident will be considered as long as those revisions are published within this market’s timeframe. However, revisions of a previously published incident impact classification of Critical (red) to another classification will not disqualify an incident from counting.

If an incident is ongoing at this market’s resolution time, the market may remain open until that incident receives an official impact classification, and it will resolve based on the first such classification published, regardless of subsequent revisions or corrections.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Discord (for example, on discordstatus.com or discordstatus.com/history); however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

Note: Discord impact classifications of Major (orange) and Critical (red) are not equivalent.

events.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Discord experiences any incident classified as Critical (red) by the listed date, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Qualifying incidents may include outages and other issues that Discord classifies as Critical.

Revisions to the impact classification of any such incident will be considered as long as those revisions are published within this market’s timeframe. However, revisions of a previously published incident impact classification of Critical (red) to another classification will not disqualify an incident from counting.

If an incident is ongoing at this market’s resolution time, the market may remain open until that incident receives an official impact classification, and it will resolve based on the first such classification published, regardless of subsequent revisions or corrections.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Discord (for example, on discordstatus.com or discordstatus.com/history); however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

Note: Discord impact classifications of Major (orange) and Critical (red) are not equivalent.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment on the "Critical Discord Incident by...?" market leans heavily toward "No," with implied probabilities hovering around 85-90% against a major disruption by year-end, driven by Discord's recent stability upgrades following mid-2024 outages tied to surging gaming traffic from hits like Black Myth: Wukong. No confirmed breaches or critical failures have surfaced since August's partial downtime, per official status pages, quelling fears amid esports season peaks. Public sentiment on platforms like Reddit and X reflects confidence in CEO Jason Citron's transparency pledges, though traders eye November's holiday traffic spikes and potential DDoS threats from rival gaming communities as key risks. Historical patterns show Discord resolves 95% of incidents within hours, favoring low-volatility bets.

eventMetadata.context_description: Carolina's commanding 3-1 series lead over the New York Islanders, fueled by Frederik Andersen's playoff mastery (3-0 record, .930 save percentage including two shutouts), drives trader consensus toward the Hurricanes ahead of critical Game 5 at UBS Arena. The Hurricanes' offense has erupted for 16 goals in their last three wins, capitalizing on Ilya Sorokin's woes (0-3, .846 SV%), while Carolina's depth shines through regular-season dominance and home-ice edge in potential closeouts. Islanders' home desperation and crowd boost could spark an upset—playoffs reward resilience—but momentum favors Carolina unless lineup changes or Sorokin's rebound alters the script.

eventMetadata.context_description: Boston Bruins enter as clear home favorites against the New Jersey Devils, with trader consensus reflecting their stronger recent form (5-2-1 in last eight) and elite goaltending from Jeremy Swayman, who's posted a .925 save percentage lately. Devils' momentum has stalled amid Jack Hughes' upper-body injury sidelining him for a third straight game, weakening their top-line scoring punch against Boston's stout penalty kill. Bruins boast a perfect 3-0 home record this season, while New Jersey struggles on the road (1-3). Watch for Devils' power play exploitation if Boston's discipline slips, but rest advantage tilts odds toward a low-scoring affair under 6.0 total goals.
